title: best herbal teas for cough
intro: Herbal teas for cough are a type of medicinal tea made from various herbs, flowers, and plants that are used to alleviate and soothe coughs and respiratory issues. These teas work by providing anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help to reduce congestion and loosen mucus, making it easier to cough up. Examples of herbal teas that can effectively treat cough ailments include peppermint tea, which eases congestion and soothes the throat, ginger tea, which reduces inflammation and calms the digestive system, and thyme tea, which possesses antibacterial properties that combat infections. Additionally, other herbal teas such as licorice root tea, slippery elm tea, and eucalyptus tea are also used to treat coughs, with licorice root tea providing a soothing and protective effect on the mucous membranes, slippery elm tea coating and soothing the throat, and eucalyptus tea opening up airways and relieving congestion.

teas.plant_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tea contains bioactive constituents such as glycyrrhizin, flavonoids, and triterpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that help to treat cough ailments by loosening mucus and reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract. The soothing and anti-inflammatory properties of this herbal tea also help to calm the throat and reduce coughing, making it an effective natural remedy for cough relief. To make this tea, combine 1 teaspoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root with 1 cup of boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes before straining and consuming. To treat a cough, drink 2-3 cups of this tea per day, ideally before bedtime, to help alleviate symptoms and promote a restful night's sleep.

Echinacea purpurea tea contains bioactive constituents such as alkylamides, glycosides, and phenolic acids, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties that help to soothe and calm the cough. The antimicrobial properties of this herbal tea, particularly the presence of alkylamides, can help to reduce the severity and frequency of coughs by combating underlying infections. To make this tea, steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and consume while still warm. Consuming 2-3 cups of Echinacea purpurea tea per day, ideally before bedtime, can help to alleviate cough symptoms and promote a restful night's sleep.

Eucalyptus globulus tea contains bioactive constituents such as eucalyptol, cineole, and alpha-pinene, which contribute to its decongestant and expectorant properties, helping to relieve cough and congestion. The antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties of this tea also aid in soothing a sore throat and reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract. To make this tea, simply steep 1 teaspoon of dried eucalyptus leaves in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink. Consuming this tea 2-3 times a day, ideally before meals, can help to effectively treat a persistent cough and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory ailments.

Thymus vulgaris tea contains bioactive constituents such as thymol and carvacrol, which possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help to alleviate cough symptoms. The expectorant properties of this herbal tea facilitate the expulsion of mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract, providing relief from coughs and congestion. To make Thymus vulgaris tea, steep 1 teaspoon of dried thyme leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and let it cool. Drinking 1-2 cups of this tea, 2-3 times a day, can help to soothe a cough and promote overall respiratory health.

Zingiber officinale tea contains bioactive constituents such as gingerol and shogaol, which have potent anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that help to treat cough ailments by reducing inflammation and loosening mucus. The properties of this herbal tea, including its warming and drying effects, also help to relieve congestion and soothe the throat, making it an effective remedy for coughs and colds. To make this tea, simply steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried ginger root in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink. To consume this tea, drink it 2-3 times a day, ideally before meals, to help alleviate cough symptoms and promote overall well-being.

Sambucus nigra tea contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, helping to alleviate cough and cold symptoms. The expectorant properties of this herbal tea help to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion. To make Sambucus nigra tea, combine 1 teaspoon of dried elderberry flowers with 1 cup of boiling water, then let it steep for 5-7 minutes before straining and consuming. To treat a cough, drink 1-2 cups of Sambucus nigra tea 2-3 times a day, ideally before bedtime to help soothe and calm the throat and respiratory system.

Lavandula angustifolia tea contains bioactive constituents such as linalool and linalyl acetate, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe and calm the throat and lungs to treat cough ailments. The antispasmodic and expectorant properties of this herbal tea help to relax the airways and promote the removal of mucus, providing relief from coughing and congestion. To make this tea, steep 1 teaspoon of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink. For optimal relief, consume this tea 2-3 times a day, either hot or cold, and sweetened with honey if desired, to help calm the cough and promote a restful night's sleep.

Verbascum thapsus tea contains bioactive constituents such as mucilage, flavonoids, and saponins that help to treat cough ailments by soothing and calming the respiratory tract. This herbal tea has expectorant properties, which aid in loosening and removing mucus and phlegm from the lungs, thereby relieving cough symptoms. To make Verbascum thapsus tea, combine 1 teaspoon of dried Verbascum thapsus flowers in a cup of boiling water, let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink. To consume this tea, drink it 2-3 times a day to help alleviate cough symptoms, and you can add honey or lemon to taste for added comfort.

Rosmarinus officinalis tea contains bioactive constituents such as carnosic acid, carnosol, and rosmarinic acid, which have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help to treat cough ailments. The herbal properties of this tea, including its antitussive and expectorant properties, aid in relieving cough symptoms by reducing inflammation and expelling mucus from the respiratory tract. To make this tea, combine 1 teaspoon of dried Rosmarinus officinalis leaves with 1 cup of boiling water, let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain and discard the solids. Consume this tea 2-3 times a day, sweetened with honey if desired, to effectively relieve cough symptoms and promote overall respiratory health.

Cinchona officinalis tea contains bioactive constituents such as quinine, alkaloids, and glycosides that help to treat cough ailments by reducing inflammation, relieving congestion, and soothing the respiratory tract. This herbal tea also possesses anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties that help to combat underlying infections and alleviate cough symptoms. To make cinchona officinalis tea for cough treatment, steep 1 teaspoon of dried cinchona bark in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and discard the solids. To consume this tea, drink 1-2 cups per day, ideally after meals, to help alleviate cough symptoms and promote overall respiratory health.

Ginkgo biloba tea contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, bilobalide, and ginkgolides, which have been found to be effective in treating cough ailments by reducing inflammation and soothing the respiratory tract. The properties of this herbal tea, including its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, help to calm the cough and provide relief from congestion. To make this tea, simply steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried ginkgo biloba leaves in a cup of hot water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink. Consume 1-2 cups of ginkgo biloba tea per day, ideally before bedtime, to help alleviate cough symptoms and promote a restful night's sleep.

Camellia sinensis tea contains various bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, theaflavins, and thearubigins, which possess potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm a cough. The mucilaginous properties of Camellia sinensis tea, particularly its high content of mucilage, also contribute to its ability to treat coughs by forming a protective barrier over the mucous membranes, thereby reducing inflammation and irritation. To make this tea, combine one teaspoon of loose-leaf Camellia sinensis tea with boiling water, let it steep for 3-5 minutes, and strain the liquid before consuming. Drinking 1-2 cups of this tea per day can help to alleviate cough symptoms, and it is best consumed warm, either on its own or with honey and lemon, to help soothe the throat and promote relaxation.

Hyptis suaveolens tea contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, saponins, and terpenoids that help to soothe and calm the throat, reducing the frequency and severity of coughs. The expectorant properties of this tea, combined with its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, aid in relieving congestion and promoting the removal of mucus from the respiratory tract. To make this tea, combine 1 teaspoon of dried Hyptis suaveolens leaves with 1 cup of boiling water, then steep for 5-7 minutes before straining and serving. To treat a cough ailment, consume 1-2 cups of Hyptis suaveolens tea 2-3 times a day, either hot or warm, to help alleviate symptoms and promote recovery.

Plantago lanceolata tea contains a rich blend of bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which possess potent anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, making it an effective remedy for treating cough ailments. The anti-inflammatory properties of Plantago lanceolata tea help to soothe and calm the irritated respiratory tract, while its expectorant properties facilitate the removal of mucus and phlegm, providing quick relief from coughing. To make this tea, simply steep 1 teaspoon of dried Plantago lanceolata leaves in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and let it cool. To treat a cough, consume 1-2 cups of Plantago lanceolata tea 2-3 times a day, either hot or lukewarm, sweetened with honey if desired, to help alleviate symptoms and promote overall respiratory health.

Ipecacuanha tea contains a plethora of bioactive constituents, including emetine and cephaline, which are responsible for its expectorant properties that help to treat cough ailments. The properties of this herbal tea, including its demulcent and anti-inflammatory properties, help to soothe and calm the irritated respiratory tract, relieving coughs and congestion. To make ipecacuanha tea, combine 1 teaspoon of dried ipecacuanha root with 1 cup of boiling water and steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain and let it cool. To consume this tea, drink it 2-3 times a day, ideally before meals, to help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract and provide relief from cough symptoms.

Taraxacum officinale tea contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, saponins, and sesquiterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These constituents help to treat cough ailment by exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and expectorant properties, which soothe the respiratory tract and facilitate the expulsion of mucus. To make this tea, steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried dandelion root and leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink. To consume this tea, drink 1-2 cups per day, ideally before meals, to help alleviate cough symptoms and promote overall respiratory health.

Achillea millefolium tea contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes that help to treat a persistent cough by reducing inflammation and combating bacteria that may be causing the illness. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties, particularly from compounds like apigenin and luteolin, soothe the throat and alleviate congestion, making it easier to breathe and reducing the urge to cough. To make this tea, steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried Achillea millefolium leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink 2-3 cups a day to help alleviate cough symptoms. Consuming this tea after meals and before bedtime can help to reduce nighttime coughing fits and promote a restful night's sleep.

Eupatorium perfoliatum tea contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids, which possess anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that help to soothe and calm a persistent cough. The tea's demulcent properties help to form a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, reducing irritation and inflammation, while its expectorant properties aid in the removal of mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract. To make Eupatorium perfoliatum tea, steep one teaspoon of dried herb in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and discard the solids. To treat a cough, consume the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally after meals, and as needed, to help alleviate symptoms and promote relaxation.

Melissa officinalis tea contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, terpenes, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. These compounds possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic properties that help to soothe a cough and alleviate respiratory tract issues. To make Melissa officinalis tea, steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried Melissa officinalis leaves in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and let it cool. Consuming this tea 2-3 times a day, either hot or cold, can help to calm a cough and provide relief from respiratory discomfort.

Origanum vulgare tea contains bioactive constituents such as carvacrol, thymol, and rosmarinic acid, which possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the respiratory tract, effectively treating cough ailments. The expectorant properties of this herbal tea help to loosen and clear mucus, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion. To make this tea, steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried Origanum vulgare leaves in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and let it cool before consumption. To treat a cough, drink 2-3 cups of Origanum vulgare tea throughout the day, ideally before meals, to help alleviate symptoms and promote recovery.
